title: Annual Breach Reporting — Trend of Affected Individuals & Root Cause

compliance.assurance_rationale: HIPAA Security §164.408 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA Security §164.408 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.10.50: Annual Breach Reporting — Trend of Affected Individuals & Root Cause.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.10.50' running on index=privacy sourcetype=hipaa:breach_closeout (close_date, affected_count, root_cause, vector), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:breach_closeout" earliest=-800d confirmed_breach="true"
| eval year=strftime(strptime(close_date,"%Y-%m-%d"),"%Y")
| stats sum(affected_count) as individuals count as incidents by year, root_cause, vector
| sort year, - individuals
description: Aggregates year-over-year breach statistics and root causes for leadership and regulatory annual summaries.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes privacy and sourcetypes hipaa:breach_closeout, hipaa:breach_closeout`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Standardize `root_cause` taxonomy (misdelivery, theft, hacking); (2) align with OCR data request fields; (3) scrub narrative PHI from free-text fields. Indexes required: `index=privacy`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_closeout`, `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_closeout``.
visualization: Stacked column (individuals by cause per year), Line chart (incident count trend).
knownFalsePositives: PHI access from authorized clinical workflows, billing operations, audit reviews, or BAA partners.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_closeout` (`close_date`, `affected_count`, `root_cause`, `vector`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Standardize `root_cause` taxonomy (misdelivery, theft, hacking); (2) align with OCR data request fields; (3) scrub narrative PHI from free-text fields.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:breach_closeout" earliest=-800d confirmed_breach="true"
| eval year=strftime(strptime(close_date,"%Y-%m-%d"),"%Y")
| stats sum(affected_count) as individuals count as incidents by year, root_cause, vector
| sort year, - individuals
```

Understanding this SPL

**Annual Breach Reporting — Trend of Affected Individuals & Root Cause (§164.408 / OCR reporting)** — Aggregates year-over-year breach statistics and root causes for leadership and regulatory annual summaries.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_closeout` (`close_date`, `affected_count`, `root_cause`, `vector`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: privacy; **sourcetype**: hipaa:breach_closeout. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=privacy, sourcetype="hipaa:breach_closeout", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **year** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by year, root_cause, vector** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the EHR audit trail (Epic, Cerner), walk known-good cases from the same workflow class, and confirm index coverage, field extractions, and access permissions match expectations.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked column (individuals by cause per year), Line chart (incident count trend).
grandmaExplanation: We aggregates year-over-year breach statistics and root causes for leadership and regulatory annual summaries. HIPAA is about keeping health records private and secure when we use them in care and operations.
